fix(docker): disable Poetry virtualenv so packages are visible to system Python (#108)

## Problem
`docker compose up` fails immediately with `ModuleNotFoundError: No
module named 'binance'`.
Poetry creates a virtualenv by default. Inside Docker, the container
command runs bare
`python buibui.py`, which uses the system Python — it cannot see
packages installed into
  the Poetry virtualenv, so all dependencies are effectively invisible.

  ## Fix
Add `poetry config virtualenvs.create false` before `poetry install` in
the Dockerfile.
This installs packages directly into the system Python, which is the
correct pattern
  for Docker + Poetry.

Also adds `--without dev` to exclude dev dependencies from the
production image
  (aligns with PR #103).

  ## Test plan
  - [ ] `docker compose up` starts without ModuleNotFoundError
- [ ] Both `price-monitor` and `position-monitor` services boot
successfully

Replace FDR calibration with Precision@K in benchmark reports

The FDR calibration plot was misleading for MAGeCK: its neg|fdr is computed
over the full ~17k-gene screen, but we evaluate on the ~1300-gene benchmark
subset (45% essential). At any nominal threshold, the benchmark-subset
empirical FDR for MAGeCK is near zero because the few genes called at low FDR
happen to all be CEGv2 -- this is a domain mismatch, not better calibration.

Precision@K is domain-invariant (rank both methods and measure fraction of
top-K that are CEGv2), making the comparison fair. Replaced in both:
- benchmark/multi_dataset_comparison.qmd
- benchmark/real_data_comparison.qmd

fix: warn when PM2 daemon runs in foreign cgroup (#302)

* fix: warn when PM2 daemon runs in foreign cgroup (#301)

After pm2 startup succeeds, check if the PM2 daemon is running inside
another service's cgroup. If so, warn the user to reboot so PM2 can
start under its own systemd unit (pm2-<user>.service).

This prevents a silent failure mode where PM2 gets killed when the
parent service (e.g., a bootstrap service) is restarted by needrestart
or systemd.

- Broken migration ordering → db:migrate from scratch fails
- schema.rb out of sync → db:schema:load fails
- Jekyll deleting static assets → asset existence check fails
- Jekyll index.html overriding Rails → index.html check fails

Runs on every PR and push to main.

Fix grid resize spill/deformation by locking height for all custom ratios

Root cause: column-only resize kept aspect-ratio: 16/9 on cells, causing
wider columns to grow disproportionately tall and explode grid height.

Key changes:
- Lock grid height mathematically (width × 9/16) for ANY custom ratio
- Strip aspect-ratio (grid-resized class) for both column and row resize
- Set explicit equal rows when only column ratio is active
- Skip custom ratios in focus mode, restore them on unfocus
- Recalculate locked height on window resize
- Hide resize handles in focus mode

fix(runner): signal file as ground truth for worker completion

Rate limit events caused false-positive success: SDK stream error
handler treated protocol handshake messages as evidence of work.
Validation auto-passed on any exception including rate limits.

Fix: (1) After SDK dispatch, verify signal file exists — no signal
means worker didn't complete regardless of SDK status. (2) Stream
errors without result_text now return failed. (3) Validation stream
errors return fail instead of auto-pass.
